title: ACP Editor Integration
summary: How to run Hermes Agent as an ACP (Agent Client Protocol) server for use inside ACP-compatible editors like VS Code, Zed, and JetBrains, enabling editor-native chat, file diffs, tool activity, and terminal commands.

cli_commands.purpose: Alias to start Hermes in ACP mode
Run first-run provider/model setup for registry clients

gotchas: ACP mode uses stdio for JSON-RPC — stdout is reserved for protocol traffic, logs go to stderr
Browser tools require Node.js 22 and ~400MB download — use --setup-browser to install
Zed registry install requires uv to be available in PATH
ACP sessions are in-memory only — list/load/resume/fork are scoped to the current running server process
The hermes-acp toolset excludes messaging delivery and cron management compared to the full toolset

`delegate_task` does NOT persist context between calls (ephemeral). For multi-step or multi-profile orchestration that needs to remember, use named ACP sessions — see community-multi-hermes-orchestration
full_text:
# ACP Editor Integration

Hermes Agent can run as an ACP server, letting ACP-compatible editors talk to Hermes over stdio.
